message.content:
In /home/aster/dev/rust/Obstruct, I need to understand the full transpilation logic for binary operators and existing TODO items. Specifically:

1. Read src/transpiler/expr_to_c.rs fully - I need to understand:
   - How binary operators (+, -, *, /, %, ==, !=, <, >, <=, >=, **, etc.) are transpiled
   - How `&&` and `||` are handled (or NOT handled) - search for "And" and "Or" expr variants
   - How `Mod` is handled (or NOT handled)
   - How the code handles different types (i32, f64, bool, char, strlit, vec)
   - How `pre_transpile` handles function parameters and generic functions
   - How `get_type` works for various expressions
   - Check if Expr::Class, Expr::Lambda exist and how they're handled

2. Read src/transpiler/code_gen_context.rs fully - understand how the C runtime is assembled

3. Search for how `use` imports are handled in the transpiler

4. Check the README for any features mentioned but not in the code

Report exact line numbers and code for all findings.

content.text: I'll conduct a thorough search of the transpilation logic. Let me start by reading the key files and searching for relevant patterns in parallel.
Now let me read the remaining transpiler file and search for additional relevant patterns.

input.description: List Expr variants in the AST

content.content:
1	use crate::error;
2	use crate::expr::Expr;
3	use crate::span::Span;
4	use crate::transpiler::code_gen_context::CodeGenContext;
5	use crate::type_env::{nil_type, Type};
6	use std::collections::{HashMap, HashSet};
7	
8	pub struct CompileTimeEnv {
9	    all_types: Vec<Type>,
10	    scopes: Vec<HashMap<String, (usize, bool, Type)>>, // variable: id, is_mutable, type
11	    current_scope: usize,
12	
13	    these: Vec<String>,
14	
15	    /// HashMap<class name, (Vec<(member name, type)>, next member id)>
16	    members: HashMap<Type, (Vec<(String, Type)>, usize)>,
17	
18	    next_var_id: usize,
19	    next_type_id: usize,
20	
21	    generic_functions: Vec<(Type, Expr)>,
22	
23	    /// Set of ref<T> type names that have already been emitted as typedefs
24	    ref_typedefs_emitted: HashSet<String>,
25	}
26	
27	impl CompileTimeEnv {

477	
478	    // Scope Management
479	
480	    pub fn push_scope(&mut self) {
481	        self.scopes.push(HashMap::new());
482	        self.current_scope += 1;
483	    }
484	
485	    pub fn pop_scope(&mut self) {
486	        self.scopes.pop();
487	        self.current_scope -= 1;
488	    }
489	
490	    // Class handling
491	
492	    pub fn declare_member(&mut self, name: String, t: Type, class_type: Type) -> usize {
493	        let class = self.members.get_mut(&class_type).unwrap();
494	        let id = class.1;
495	
496	        class.0.push((name, t));
497	
498	        id
499	    }
500	
501	    pub fn c_member_name(&mut self, ty: &Type, name: &str, span: Span) -> String {
502	        let class = self.members.get(ty);
503	
504	        match class {
505	            Some(class) => {
506	                format!("m_{}", class.1)
507	            }
508	            None => {
509	                error(
510	                    span,
511	                    &format!(
512	                        "Could not find type '{}' when fetching member '{}'.",
513	                        ty, name
514	                    ),
515	                    "fetching mangled member name",
516	                );
517	                String::new()
518	            }
519	        }
520	    }
521	
522	    pub fn get_member_type(&self, ty: &Type, name: &str) -> Option<Type> {
523	        let class = self.members.get(ty);
524	        match class {
525	            Some(class) => {
526	                let mut output = None;
527	                for member in class.0.iter() {
528	                    if member.0 == name {
529	                        output = Some(member.1.clone());
530	                        break;
531	                    }
532	                }
533	                output
534	            }
535	            None => None,
536	        }
537	    }
538	
539	    pub fn register_class(&mut self, ty: Type) -> usize {
540	        self.members.insert(ty.clone(), (vec![], 0));
541	        self.register_type(ty.clone())
542	    }
543	
544	    // Variable Handling
545	
546	    pub fn declare_var(&mut self, name: String, is_mutable: bool, var_type: Type) -> usize {
547	        self.declare_var_inner(name, is_mutable, var_type)
548	    }
549	
550	    fn declare_var_inner(&mut self, name: String, is_mutable: bool, var_type: Type) -> usize {
551	        let id = self.next_var_id;
552	        self.next_var_id += 1;
553	
554	        let scope = self.scopes.last_mut().unwrap();
555	        scope.insert(name, (id, is_mutable, var_type));
556	
557	        id
558	    }
559	
560	    pub fn declare_global_var(&mut self, name: String, is_mutable: bool, var_type: Type) -> usize {
561	        let id = self.next_var_id;
562	        self.next_var_id += 1;
563	
564	        let scope = self.scopes.first_mut().unwrap();
565	        scope.insert(name, (id, is_mutable, var_type));
566	
567	        id
568	    }
569	
570	    fn resolve_var(&self, name: &str) -> Option<(usize, usize)> {
571	        for (idx, scope) in self.scopes.iter().enumerate().rev() {
572	            if let Some(id) = scope.get(name) {
573	                return Some((id.0, idx));
574	            }
575	        }
576	        None
577	    }
578	
579	    pub fn get_var(&self, name: &str) -> Option<(bool, Type)> {
580	        for scope in self.scopes.iter().rev() {
581	            if let Some((_id, is_mutable, var_type)) = scope.get(name) {
582	                return Some((*is_mutable, var_type.clone()));
583	            }
584	        }
585	        None
586	    }
587	
588	    pub fn var_exists(&self, name: &str) -> bool {
589	        for scope in self.scopes.iter().rev() {
590	            if let Some(_) = scope.get(name) {
591	                return true;
592	            }
593	        }
594	        false
595	    }
596	
597	    /// Returns the C variable name for variables.\
598	    /// Format: v_{id}s_{scope}
599	    pub fn c_var_name(&self, name: &str, span: Span) -> String {
600	        let (id, scope) = self.resolve_var(name).unwrap_or_else(|| {
601	            error(
602	                span,
603	                format!("Could not find variable '{}'", name).as_str(),
604	                "fetching variable name",
605	            );
606	            (0, 0)
607	        });
608	        format!("v_{}s_{}", id, scope)
609	    }
610	
611	    /// Returns the C variable name for a function instance with generics.\
612	    /// Format: v_{id}s_{scope}C{generic_types}D for generic functions, v_{id}s_{scope}CD for non-generic
613	    pub fn c_func_instance_name(&mut self, name: &str, generics: &[Type], span: Span) -> String {
614	        let (id, scope) = self.resolve_var(name).unwrap_or_else(|| {
615	            error(
616	                span,
617	                format!("Could not find variable '{}'", name).as_str(),
618	                "fetching function name",
619	            );
620	            (0, 0)
621	        });
622	
623	        let mut result = format!("v_{}s_{}", id, scope);
624	
625	        if generics.is_empty() {
626	            // Non-generic function: add CD suffix
627	            result.push_str("CD");
628	        } else {
629	            // Match inline convention: C opens the mangling bracket,
630	            // each type uses its raw name (e.g. t_0 for i32), types
631	            // separated by C, ending with DD (= last type's CD + closing D).
632	            // e.g. v_1s_0Ct_0Ct_0CDD for _add(i32, i32)
633	            result.push('C');
634	            for g in generics.iter() {
635	                result.push_str(&self.c_type_name_raw(g, span));
636	                result.push('C');
637	            }
638	            result.push_str("DD");
639	        }
640	
641	        result
642	    }
643	
644	    // Type Handling
645	
646	    pub fn register_type(&mut self, ty: Type) -> usize {
647	        if let Type::Conceptual(name) = &ty {
648	            panic!("Tried to register conceptual type: {}", name);
649	        }
650	
651	        let canonical_ty = self.canonicalize_type(ty);
652	
653	        if let Some(index) = self.all_types.iter().position(|t| t == &canonical_ty) {
654	            return index;
655	        }
656	
657	        let id = self.next_type_id;
658	        self.next_type_id += 1;
659	
660	        self.all_types.push(canonical_ty);
661	        id
662	    }
663	
664	    /// Returns the ID of a type. If the type is generic, it registers its generic types
665	    fn get_type_id(&mut self, ty: &Type) -> Option<usize> {
666	        let canonical = self.canonicalize_type(ty.clone());
667	        if !ty.is_conceptual() {
668	            self.all_types.iter().position(|t| t == &canonical)
669	        } else {
670	            self.all_types
671	                .iter()
672	                .position(|t| t.name() == ty.name() && !t.has_generics())
673	        }
674	    }
675	
676	    /// Registers the generic types in a generic type
677	    fn canonicalize_type(&mut self, ty: Type) -> Type {
678	        let canonical_generics: Vec<Type> = ty
679	            .generics()
680	            .iter()
681	            .map(|g| {
682	                let id = self.register_type(g.clone());
683	                self.all_types[id].clone()
684	            })
685	            .collect();
686	
687	        if canonical_generics.is_empty() {
688	            ty
689	        } else {
690	            Type::with_generics(ty.name(), canonical_generics)
691	        }
692	    }
693	
694	    /// Internal helper that generates the type name without the CD suffix
695	    fn c_type_name_raw(&mut self, ty: &Type, span: Span) -> String {
696	        // Check if this is a function type (has "func" as its name)
697	        if ty.name() == "func" {
698	            // For function types, use the mangled format: t_5C{param_types,return_type}D
699	            let gens = ty.generics();
700	            let mut name = String::from("t_5C");
701	
702	            // Add all generics (args + return type) separated by underscore
703	            for (i, g) in gens.iter().enumerate() {
704	                name.push_str(&self.c_type_name_raw(g, span));
705	                if i != gens.len() - 1 {
706	                    name.push('_');
707	                }
708	            }
709	
710	            // Close with D
711	            name.push('D');
712	
713	            name
714	        } else {
715	            // For non-function types, use the standard ID-based naming.
716	            // For generic types, find the base type's ID (without generics)
717	            // instead of the position of the full canonicalized type in all_types.
718	            // e.g. ref<i32> should use the base ref type's ID (t_7), not the
719	            // position of the instantiated type (which could be t_16).
720	            let base_id = self
721	                .all_types
722	                .iter()
723	                .position(|t| t.name() == ty.name() && !t.has_generics());
724	            let type_id = if let Some(id) = base_id {
725	                id
726	            } else {
727	                self.get_type_id(ty).unwrap_or_else(|| {
728	                    error(
729	                        span,
730	                        format!("Could not find type '{}'", ty).as_str(),
731	                        "transpiling",
732	                    );
733	                    0
734	                })
735	            };
736	
737	            let mut name = format!("t_{}", type_id);
738	
739	            let gens = ty.generics();
740	            if !gens.is_empty() {
741	                name.push('C');
742	
743	                for (i, g) in gens.iter().enumerate() {
744	                    name.push_str(&self.c_type_name_raw(g, span));
745	                    if i != gens.len() - 1 {
746	                        name.push('_');
747	                    }
748	                }
749	
750	                name.push('D');
751	            }
752	
753	            name
754	        }
755	    }
756	
757	    /// Public API: returns type name with CD suffix for non-generic consistency.\
758	    /// For function types, the mangling already ends with D, so no additional CD.\
759	    /// For non-function types, add CD suffix.\
760	    /// \
761	    /// Format for simple types: t_{id}\
762	    /// Format for generic types and for simple functions: t_{id}C{generic types}D\
763	    /// Format for generic functions: t_{id}C{argument types, return type}DC{generic types}D\
764	    /// ^^^Note that generic types, argument types and return type are separated by "_".
765	    pub fn c_type_name(&mut self, ty: &Type, ctx: &mut CodeGenContext, span: Span) -> String {
766	        let name = self.c_type_name_raw(ty, span);
767	
768	        // For function types, the raw name already ends with D, so don't add CD
769	        if ty.name() == "func" {
770	            name
771	        } else if ty.generics().is_empty() {
772	            let mut result = name;
773	            result.push_str("CD");
774	            result
775	        } else if ty.name() == "ref" {
776	            // Ensure a typedef like: typedef t_15CD* t_7Ct_15CD; exists for ref<SomeType>
777	            let inner_type = ty.generics()[0].clone();
778	            let inner_raw = self.c_type_name_raw(&inner_type, span);
779	            let inner_c_type = if inner_type.name() == "func" {
780	                inner_raw
781	            } else {
782	                inner_raw + "CD"
783	            };
784	            let typedef_name = name.clone();
785	            let typedef_key = format!("ref_{}", inner_c_type);
786	            if !self.ref_typedefs_emitted.contains(&typedef_key) {
787	                self.ref_typedefs_emitted.insert(typedef_key);
788	                ctx.types
789	                    .push_str(&format!("typedef {}* {};\n", inner_c_type, typedef_name));
790	            }
791	            name
792	        } else {
793	            name
794	        }
795	    }
796	
797	    pub fn add_func_type(
798	        &mut self,
799	        ret_type: Type,
800	        arg_types: Vec<Type>,
801	        ctx: &mut CodeGenContext,
802	        span: Span,
803	    ) {
804	        // Generate the function type name using the mangling scheme
805	        // Format: t_5C{args, ret}DC{gens}D
806	        let mut gens = arg_types.clone();
807	        gens.push(ret_type.clone());
808	        let func_type = Type::with_generics("func", gens);
809	
810	        if self.get_type_id(&func_type).is_some() {
811	            return;
812	        }
813	
814	        self.register_type(func_type.clone());
815	
816	        let func_type_name = self.c_type_name(&func_type, ctx, span);
817	        let ret_type_name = self.c_type_name(&ret_type, ctx, span);
818	
819	        // Generate the typedef for the function type
820	        let mut buf = String::from("typedef ");
821	        buf.push_str(&ret_type_name);
822	        buf.push_str(&format!("(*{})", func_type_name));
823	        buf.push('(');
824	        for ty in arg_types.iter() {
825	            buf.push_str(&self.c_type_name(ty, ctx, span));
826	            buf.push(',');
827	        }
828	        if arg_types.len() >= 1 {
829	            buf.pop();
830	        }
831	        buf.push_str(");\n");
832	        ctx.types.push_str(&buf);
833	    }
834	
835	    pub fn push_this(&mut self, this: &str) {
836	        self.these.push(this.to_string());
837	    }
838	    pub fn pop_this(&mut self) {
839	        self.these.pop();
840	    }
841	    pub fn this(&self) -> &str {
842	        &self.these.last().unwrap()
843	    }
844	
845	    pub fn del_var(&mut self, name: &str) {
846	        for scope in self.scopes.iter_mut().rev() {
847	            if let Some(_id) = scope.get(name) {
848	                scope.remove(name);
849	            }
850	        }
851	    }
852	
853	    /// Returns true if the type is a registered class (struct type)
854	    pub fn is_class(&self, ty: &Type) -> bool {
855	        self.members.contains_key(ty)
856	    }
857	
858	    /// Returns the C type name for a parameter, using pointer for structs
859	    pub fn c_param_type(&mut self, ty: &Type, ctx: &mut CodeGenContext, span: Span) -> String {
860	        if self.is_class(ty) {
861	            format!("{}*", self.c_type_name(ty, ctx, span))
862	        } else {
863	            self.c_type_name(ty, ctx, span)
864	        }
865	    }
866	
867	    pub fn complete(&mut self, _ctx: &mut CodeGenContext) {
868	        todo!("This function is currently not implemented");
869	    }
870	}
871

1	use crate::runtime_env::RuntimeEnvironment;
2	use crate::span::Span;
3	use crate::type_env::Type;
4	use crate::value::Value;
5	
6	#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
7	pub enum UseKind {
8	    Normal,
9	    Std,
10	}
11	#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
12	pub enum Expr {
13	    Nothing(),
14	    // Literals
15	    Float(f64),
16	    Int(i32),
17	    Bool(bool),
18	    Str(String),
19	    Char(String),
20	    Vector(Vec<Expr>),
21	    Array(Vec<Expr>),
22	
23	    // Binary Operators
24	    Add(Box<Expr>, Box<Expr>, Span),
25	    Sub(Box<Expr>, Box<Expr>, Span),
26	    Mult(Box<Expr>, Box<Expr>, Span),
27	    Div(Box<Expr>, Box<Expr>, Span),
28	    Mod(Box<Expr>, Box<Expr>, Span),
29	    Power(Box<Expr>, Box<Expr>, Span),
30	    EqualEqual(Box<Expr>, Box<Expr>, Span),
31	    BangEqual(Box<Expr>, Box<Expr>, Span),
32	    GreaterEqual(Box<Expr>, Box<Expr>, Span),
33	    LessEqual(Box<Expr>, Box<Expr>, Span),
34	    Less(Box<Expr>, Box<Expr>, Span),
35	    Greater(Box<Expr>, Box<Expr>, Span),
36	    And(Box<Expr>, Box<Expr>, Span),
37	    Or(Box<Expr>, Box<Expr>, Span),
38	
39	    Nth(Box<Expr>, Box<Expr>, Span),
40	
41	    // Unary Operators
42	    Not(Box<Expr>, Span),
43	    Ref(Box<Expr>, Span),
44	    Deref(Box<Expr>, Span),
45	
46	    // Statements
47	    StmtBlockWithScope(Vec<Box<Expr>>, Span),
48	    StmtBlock(Vec<Box<Expr>>, Span),
49	    Print(Box<Expr>, Span),
50	    Discard(Box<Expr>),
51	    Stmt(Box<Expr>),
52	
53	    // Functions
54	    DeclareFunction(
55	        String,
56	        Box<Expr>,
57	        Option<Type>,
58	        Vec<(String, Type)>,
59	        Vec<String>,
60	        Span,
61	    ),
62	    Function(Box<Expr>, Type, Vec<(String, Type)>, Vec<String>),
63	    /// function, generics, args, span
64	    CallFunc(String, Vec<Type>, Vec<Box<Expr>>, Span),
65	    Return(Box<Expr>, Span),
66	
67	    // Variables
68	    Variable(String, Span),
69	    Declare(String, Option<Type>, Option<Box<Expr>>, bool, Span),
70	    Assign(Box<Expr>, Box<Expr>, Span),
71	    Delete(String),
72	    This(Span),
73	
74	    // Control Flow

76	    If(Box<Expr>, Box<Expr>, Option<Box<Expr>>, bool),
77	    While(Box<Expr>, Box<Expr>),
78	    /// loopee, looper, block
79	    For(String, Box<Expr>, Box<Expr>, Span),
80	
81	    // Data Structures
82	    /// new class, Vec<(member name, type)>, span
83	    Class(Type, Vec<(String, Type)>, Span),
84	    /// value, member, span
85	    Member(Box<Expr>, String, Span),
86	
87	    // Others
88	    Custom(fn(&mut RuntimeEnvironment) -> Value),
89	    Custom2(fn(&mut RuntimeEnvironment, Vec<Value>) -> Value),
90	    Value(Value),
91	    Use {
92	        kind: UseKind,

input.description: Find TODOs in parser and scanner

content.text:
Now I have all the information needed. Here is the comprehensive report:

---

## Report: Transpilation Logic for Binary Operators and Existing TODO Items

### 1. Binary Operator Transpilation (`/home/aster/dev/rust/Obstruct/src/transpiler/expr_to_c.rs`)

Every binary operator is transpiled by wrapping the operands in a `CallFunc` that calls a type-dispatched function. The left operand's type determines which overload is selected.

**`+` (Add)** — lines 102-122:
- Gets `left_type` and `right_type`
- Special case: if left is `strlit` and right is `char`, calls `v_1s_0Ct_6Ct_4Ct_4CDD(...)` (the strlit+char concatenation helper) directly
- Otherwise, wraps in `Expr::CallFunc("_add", vec![left_type], vec![l, r], span)`
- The `_add` C functions are in `code_gen_context.rs` lines 78-100: typed overloads for `i32` (`v_1s_0Ct_0Ct_0CDD`), `f64` (`v_1s_0Ct_2Ct_2CDD`), and `strlit` (`v_1s_0Ct_6Ct_6CDD`)

**`-` (Sub)** — lines 156-165: Wraps in `Expr::CallFunc("_sub", vec![left_type], vec![l, r], span)`

**`*` (Mult)** — lines 166-175: Wraps in `Expr::CallFunc("_mult", vec![left_type], vec![l, r], span)`

**`/` (Div)** — lines 124-133: Wraps in `Expr::CallFunc("_div", vec![left_type], vec![l, r], span)`

**`%` (Mod)** — **NOT HANDLED in `to_c()`**. `Expr::Mod` exists in the AST (`/home/aster/dev/rust/Obstruct/src/expr.rs` line 28), is parsed by the parser (line 544 of `parser.rs`), and its `Span` is handled in `get_span()` (line 876 of `expr_to_c.rs`). However, there is no `Expr::Mod` arm in the `to_c()` match. It would hit the catch-all `panic!` at line 581.

**`**` (Power)** — lines 146-155: Wraps in `Expr::CallFunc("_pow", vec![left_type], vec![l, r], span)`. The C runtime only has `f64` overloads (lines 139-145 of `code_gen_context.rs`), even though `get_type()` resolves `left_type` which could be anything.

**`==` (EqualEqual)** — lines 176-185: Wraps in `Expr::CallFunc("_equal", vec![left_type], vec![l, r], span)`

**`!=` (BangEqual)** — lines 218-227: Wraps in `Expr::CallFunc("_bang_equal", vec![left_type], vec![l, r], span)`

**`<` (Less)** — lines 135-144: Wraps in `Expr::CallFunc("_less", vec![left_type], vec![l, r], span)`

**`>` (Greater)** — lines 186-195: Wraps in `Expr::CallFunc("_greater", vec![left_type], vec![l, r], span)`

**`<=` (LessEqual)** — lines 207-216: Wraps in `Expr::CallFunc("_less_equal", vec![left_type], vec![l, r], span)`

**`>=` (GreaterEqual)** — lines 196-205: Wraps in `Expr::CallFunc("_greater_equal", vec![left_type], vec![l, r], span)`

### 2. `&&` and `||` Handling

They ARE handled:

**`||` (Or)** — lines 234-238:
```rust
Expr::Or(l, r, span) => {
    Expr::CallFunc("or".into(), vec![], vec![l.clone(), r.clone()], *span)
        .to_c(cte, ctx);
    false
}
```

**`&&` (And)** — lines 240-244:
```rust
Expr::And(l, r, span) => {
    Expr::CallFunc("and".into(), vec![], vec![l.clone(), r.clone()], *span)
        .to_c(cte, ctx);
    false
}
```

The README claimed these were "not yet transpiled," but this is outdated. They are registered in the runtime environment (`compiletime_env.rs` lines 397-433) with `bool` -> `bool` overloads, and the C implementations are in `code_gen_context.rs` lines 241-247 (`v_15s_0CD` for `or`, `v_16s_0CD` for `and`).

### 3. Type Handling (`get_type()` at lines 741-829)

The `get_type()` method resolves expression types as follows:

| Expr variant | Returned type |
|---|---|
| `Int` | `"i32"` |
| `Float` | `"f64"` |
| `Str` | `"strlit"` |
| `Bool`, `EqualEqual`, `BangEqual`, `Less`, `LessEqual`, `Greater`, `GreaterEqual` | `"bool"` |
| `Char` | `"char"` |
| `Add`, `Sub`, `Mult`, `Power`, `Div` | Type of left operand |
| `Return` | `nil_type()` |
| `Nth` (array index) | `"char"` |
| `CallFunc` | Last generic (return type) of the function type |
| `StmtBlock`, `StmtBlockWithScope` | Type of last expression |
| `Variable` | Looked up from CTE scope |
| `Discard` | `nil_type()` |
| `Print` | Type of inner expr |
| `If` | Type of the if-block |
| `Member` | Type from the class member registry |
| `Ref` | `Type::with_generics("ref", vec![inner_type])` |
| `Deref` | First generic of the ref type |

Notably, `get_type()` does NOT handle: `Mod`, `And`, `Or`, `Vector`, `Array`, `Function`, `For`, `Input`, `Custom`, `Custom2`, `Value`, `Use`, `Class`, `Lambda` (which becomes `Function`). These would hit the `panic!` at line 827: `panic!("unexpected expression (for type check) '{:?}'", self)`.

### 4. `pre_transpile` — Function Parameters and Generics (`/home/aster/dev/rust/Obstruct/src/transpiler/expr_to_c.rs`, lines 585-739)

`pre_transpile` runs before code generation and handles:

- **`Expr::DeclareFunction`** (lines 593-658): Registers function type in CTE, declares parameters in a new scope, processes the body to register local variables, infers return type from `returned_type()`, creates a function declaration stub (`ctx.declarations`), and creates a `func` type with generics `[arg_types..., return_type]`.
  - Generic parameters (`_gens`) are accepted but not used in the current implementation (the field exists but is ignored).
  - The function is declared as a global variable with type `func<arg1, arg2, ..., return>`.

- **`Expr::Use`** (lines 659-694): Resolves the file path (using `STD_PATH` for stdlib imports or relative for normal imports), checks the file exists, recursively parses it (scanner -> parser), and calls `pre_transpile` on the imported AST so its functions are registered.

- **`Expr::Class`** (lines 706-723): Registers the class type, declares members, creates forward declaration and typedef in `ctx.include` and `ctx.types`.

- **`Expr::Declare`** (lines 725-735): Registers variables with their types (inferred or explicit).

- **`Expr::StmtBlock` / `StmtBlockWithScope`** (lines 696-700): Iterates over child expressions.

### 5. `Expr::Class` and `Expr::Lambda`

**`Expr::Class`** — Handled in both `to_c()` (lines 523-546) and `pre_transpile()` (lines 706-723). In `to_c()`, it generates the full C `struct` definition and writes it to `ctx.types`.

**`Expr::Function`** (the lambda AST variant) — **NOT handled in `to_c()` or `pre_transpile()`**. The parser creates `Expr::Function(body, return_type, parameters, generic_params)` at `parser.rs` line 421. It has no arm in the `to_c()` match, so it would panic at line 581. There is no `Expr::Lambda` variant in the enum; lambdas are represented as `Expr::Function`.

### 6. Code Gen Context Assembly (`/home/aster/dev/rust/Obstruct/src/transpiler/code_gen_context.rs`)

The `CodeGenContext` struct holds 5 string buffers:
- `include` — C preprocessor includes
- `types` — typedefs and struct definitions
- `declarations` — function forward declarations
- `unnamed` — unused buffer (empty)
- `body` — main function body

The `combine()` method (lines 29-304) assembles the final C program in this order:
1. `base_include` — stdlib includes (lines 30-52), with type aliases (`t_0CD` = i32, `t_1CD` = arr, `t_2CD` = f64, `t_3CD` = bool, `t_4CD` = char, `t_6CD` = strlit)
2. `base_body` — All C runtime helper functions (lines 54-283):
   - Print: `v_0s_0Ct_{i}CDD` for i32, f64, bool, strlit
   - Add: `v_1s_0Ct_{i}CDD` for i32, f64, strlit, strlit+char
   - Less: `v_2s_0Ct_{i}CDD` for i32, f64
   - Sub: `v_3s_0Ct_{i}CDD` for i32, f64
   - Mult: `v_4s_0Ct_{i}CDD` for i32, f64
   - Pow: `v_5s_0Ct_{i}CDD` for i32, f64 (both call C `pow()`)
   - Div: `v_6s_0Ct_{i}CDD` for i32, f64
   - Input: `v_7s_0CD` (intput), `v_8s_0CD` (fput), `v_9s_0CD` (strput)
   - Equal: `v_10s_0Ct_{i}CDD` for i32, f64, strlit, char
   - Greater: `v_11s_0Ct_{i}CDD` for i32, f64
   - BangEqual: `v_12s_0Ct_{i}CDD` for i32, f64, strlit, char
   - GreaterEqual: `v_13s_0Ct_{i}CDD` for i32, f64, char
   - LessEqual: `v_14s_0Ct_{i}CDD` for i32, f64
   - Or: `v_15s_0CD`
   - And: `v_16s_0CD`
   - Not: `v_17s_0CD`
   - Terminal width/height: `v_18s_0CD`, `v_19s_0CD`
   - get_char: `v_20s_0CD`
   - sleep: `v_21s_0CD`
3. `include` — User-defined forward struct declarations
4. `types` — User-defined struct definitions and type aliases
5. `declarations` — Function prototype declarations
6. `unnamed` — Empty
7. `body` — `main()` function that calls `v_...CD()` for the user's `main` function

The final assembled output has this structure:
```
#includes
typedefs + operator implementations

[user struct typedefs]

[user struct definitions]

[function declarations]

int main() {
    main();
}
```

### 7. `use` Import Handling (`Expr::Use`)

**In `to_c()`** (line 521): `Expr::Use { .. } => false` — produces no C code, just returns false (no semicolon). The import processing happens entirely in `pre_transpile()`.

**In `pre_transpile()`** (lines 659-694):
- Resolves `UseKind::Std` path as `{STD_PATH}{path}` (e.g., `std/math.obs`)
- Resolves `UseKind::Normal` path as `{current_file_dir}/{path}`
- Checks the resolved path exists
- If not already processed (tracked in `programs_to_transpile` HashMap), reads the file, scans/tokens, parses, and recursively calls `pre_transpile()` on the imported AST
- This ensures imported functions are registered before the importing file's function declarations

**UseKind enum** (`/home/aster/dev/rust/Obstruct/src/expr.rs` lines 7-10):
```rust
pub enum UseKind {
    Normal,
    Std,
}
```

### 8. Missing/Incomplete Expr Variants in Transpilation

The following `Expr` variants from the AST are **not handled** in `to_c()` and would trigger the `panic!` at line 581:

| Expr variant | Line in expr.rs | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `Mod` | 28 | `%` operator — parsed but no `to_c()` arm |
| `Function` | 62 | Lambdas (`lam { }`) — no `to_c()` or `pre_transpile()` arm |
| `Vector` | 20 | `\{1, 2, 3}` syntax — not transpiled |
| `Array` | 21 | `[1, 2, 3]` syntax — not transpiled |
| `For` | 79 | `for i : expr { }` loop — not transpiled |
| `Input` | 96 | Input statement — not transpiled |
| `Custom` | 88 | Runtime custom function — not transpiled |
| `Custom2` | 89 | Runtime custom 2-arg function — not transpiled |
| `Value` | 90 | Raw value — not transpiled |

### 9. README Features Not Present in Code

The README at `/home/aster/dev/rust/Obstruct/README.md` mentions:

1. **"Reference Syntax"** (`&` and `*` operators): Lines 121-127 claim `&name` resolves to `ref::new(name)` and `*expr` resolves to `ref::deref(expr)`. In the code, `Expr::Ref` (line 559-563 of `expr_to_c.rs`) and `Expr::Deref` (line 564-569) simply emit `&` and `*` in C syntax directly — they do NOT call `ref::new` or `ref::deref`. The README claim is inaccurate.

2. **"`**` — exponentiation (`f64` only, uses C `pow()`)**": The README says `**` works only for `f64`, but the transpilation at `expr_to_c.rs` line 146-155 passes `left_type` which could be any type. The C runtime only has `i32` and `f64` overloads for `_pow` (lines 139-145 of `code_gen_context.rs`), and `i32` overloads use `pow()` from `<math.h>` which converts to double.

3. **"`&&` / `||` (and / or) are parsed but not yet transpiled"**: Outdated — they ARE transpiled as function calls (`and`, `or`), registered in `compiletime_env.rs`, and have C implementations.
